commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because resolving the P1 finding is an API/security-boundary product choice, not a mechanical branch cleanup.; Needs attention: No supply-chain change was found, but the diff intentionally expands plugin-visible trusted usage metadata and needs maintainer boundary acceptance.; Review finding: [P1] Keep standalone usage-ledger services able to subscribe — src/plugins/services.ts:125-130 (sha=c0f10b274f5bb1d76b6aa858f10c050e3003af3b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an opt-in `plugins.modelUsage.enabled` config key, a model-usage event hub, a `ctx.modelUsage.onEvent` service context API, docs/generated baselines, and routes ingress, auto-reply, and cron usage emissions through the hub.

PR surface: Source +201, Tests +230, Docs +46, Generated 0. Total +477 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows a service whose plugin registers no providers receives `ctx.modelUsage` but its listener drops every provider event because the provider-id set is empty. This is feature-review source proof rather than a user bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because resolving the P1 finding is an API/security-boundary product choice, not a mechanical branch cleanup.

target.title: feat: expose model usage to plugin services
target.body:
Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/97149

## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw computes trusted model usage token counts and estimated cost, but plugin services do not have a narrow supported way to consume that accounting stream. Plugins that want usage ledgers, spend reports, quota tracking, or cost dashboards have to reach for broad diagnostics surfaces or live in core.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a small plugin service contract: Gateway-started services receive `ctx.modelUsage?.onEvent(...)` when operators explicitly enable `plugins.modelUsage.enabled: true`. The stream carries core-owned model usage accounting with provider/model/session attribution, token buckets, context facts, duration, and `costUsd` when available.

The stream is backed by a core-only model usage hub. Existing `model.usage` diagnostics are preserved and remain controlled by `diagnostics.enabled`, but plugin model usage delivery is separately opt-in and does not require diagnostics. Public/plugin-forgeable diagnostic emitters do not feed the plugin accounting stream, and raw prompts, responses, tool payloads, and other diagnostics are not exposed.

## User Impact

Plugin authors can build token accounting and spend/quota plugins outside the OpenClaw repo. This enables the tokenomics-style ecosystem from PR 97149 without bundling a large in-repo plugin or granting broad internal diagnostics access.

AI-assisted: yes, Codex.

## Evidence

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/services.test.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ingress-diagnostics.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.diagnostic-events.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-sdk-package-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json`
- `node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/agents/agent-command.ingress-diagnostics.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts src/infra/model-usage-events.ts src/plugins/services.test.ts src/plugins/services.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base origin/main` clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
